text.heading: Ebonyi may not benefit from Global Funds
text.p: The Ebonyi State co-ordinator of Action Committee on AIDS (EBOSACA), Augustine Nwazunku has said that with the status of health facilities, the problem of the HIV/AIDS would remain a mirage.
He said the state risks not benefiting from the Global Funds support billed to begin next year if the condition of the health facilities were not improved upon.
He made the remarks in Abakaliki the state capital to mark this year’s World AIDS Day.
Nwazunku explained that one of the conditions for accessing the Global Fund is that the benefiting state must met the standard of its health facilities.

"Most of our health facilities, for now, are short of that requirement, so we are afraid that if something significant is not done to bring those health facilities to the stipulated standard, we may miss out", he stated.

The co-ordinator said that though access to HIV drugs in the state has improved, they still have problems of service delivery especially in the areas of prevention of transmission of HIV/AIDS from mother to child.
He said that the state has dropped from its 9.8 per cent prevalence in 1999 to 4.4 per cent this year.
Nwazunku said that to ensure effective sensitisation of the rural populace, both local action committee and the community action committee had been inaugurated in the state.

He regretted that the cases of children orphaned by AIDS is on the increase, pointing out that the negative effects of the virus on the society is much, and there is need for collaboration of all stakeholders.
The EBOSACA boss lamented that though the government was doing relatively much, but'the private sector operators have kept a distance'.

He praised the state government for its commitment to the war against the HIV-AIDS, saying that a draft bill tailored to give legal lift to the EBOSACA was already in the state house of assembly, and has the blessing of the executive arm of the government.
